_doc: COLLISION SENTRY — permanent, halt-not-warn named-pair identity assertions (DECISIONS §29, owner-ruled 2026-07-09). Data-driven and EXTENSIBLE: pairs are ADDED ONLY WHEN THEY BITE (no speculative list). one_source_selftest.py loads this file and, for every pair, asserts against the single store: (1) each member key exists EXACTLY ONCE, (2) every pinned field == the store's value (a bleed makes stk._by=2007 != pinned 2000 -> FAIL), (3) the `distinct_fields` still differ between the two members (defence-in-depth: a symmetric swap that dodges the pin still trips this). Any merge/swap/bleed FAILS the build. First (and only) entry: the two Max Kings — the pair that has cross-bled ~15 times; the 2007-born Sydney player was renamed 'Maxwell King' (`max-king-syd`) specifically to stop it, and stk's birth fields were later found borrowed from syd (the corruption this sentry now guards). Wired into the suite CI already runs (one_source_selftest.py).

pairs.note: The two Max Kings (DECISIONS §29). Added because it BIT: max-king-stk borrowed max-king-syd's _by/_bd (2007-01-09) — corrected to the true St Kilda DOB 7 Jul 2000 this job.
